title: inula verbascifolia
intro: Inula verbascifolia, also known as the mullein-leaved elecampane, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin conditions, thanks to its rich content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Inula verbascifolia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide relief from respiratory and digestive issues.
